intent:
We need a goods-for-payment settlement between a Buyer and a Seller, with an
Escrow, a Shipper, and an Inspector. The Buyer will release payment only once
the goods have shipped, and the Seller will ship the goods only once payment
has been made. An Escrow service holds funds and a Shipper moves the goods; an
Inspector checks the delivered goods before the deal is finalised. Distinguish
'domestic' shipments (no customs) from 'international' shipments (which require
a customs clearance step). The settlement is complete only after the goods are
delivered, inspected, and payment is released to the Seller.
